variables.description: Whether the constitution provides for a right to life
Whether the constitution prohibits slavery, servitude, or forced labor
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Universally Prohibited; 2=Prohibited Except in the Case of War; 3=Prohibited with Other Exception(s) Noted (e.g. court-ordered labor, cases provided by law); 4=Explicitly Allowed; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified

variables.description: Whether the constitution prohibits torture
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Universally Prohibited; 2=Prohibited Except in the Case of War; 3=Prohibited for the Purpose of Extracting Confessions; 4=Explicitly Allowed; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified

variables.description: Whether the constitution prohibits cruel, inhuman, or degrading treatment
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Universally Prohibited; 2=Prohibited Except in the Case of War; 3=Explicitly Allowed; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified

variables.description: Whether the constitution provides for a right of privacy (includes domicile/home privacy, correspondence, communications; 'domicile is inviolable' qualifies)
Whether the constitution provides for freedom of movement
Whether the constitution provides for freedom of opinion, thought, and/or conscience (distinct from freedom of religion; specifically the internal freedom of thought, not expression)
Whether the constitution provides for freedom of expression or speech (freedom of the press alone is not sufficient; expression or speech must be explicitly mentioned)
Whether the constitution provides for a right of petition (right to submit individual or group grievances to government; includes complaints to state organs about official transgressions)
Whether the constitution prohibits censorship
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes, universally prohibited; 2=Censorship allowed in exceptional cases (war, state of emergency, public safety); 3=No prohibition; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine

variables.description: Whether the constitution provides for freedom of the press
Whether the constitution refers to any international human rights treaties or instruments. Multi-response.
variables.coding_scheme: Sub-variables (each 0=not selected, 1=selected, 99=not applicable): INTRGHT_1=UN Universal Declaration of Human Rights (1948); INTRGHT_2=1789 French Declaration of Rights; INTRGHT_3=Article 45, UN Charter (1945); INTRGHT_4=European Convention for the Protection of Human Rights and Fundamental Freedoms (1950); INTRGHT_5=International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights (1966); INTRGHT_6=International Covenant on Economic and Social Rights (1966); INTRGHT_7=American Convention on Human Rights (1969); INTRGHT_8=Helsinki Accords (1966); INTRGHT_9=African Charter on Human People's Rights (1981); INTRGHT_96=Other; INTRGHT_97=Unable to Determine; INTRGHT_98=Not Specified

variables.description: Whether the constitution provides for freedom of assembly
Whether the constitution provides for freedom of association
Whether the constitution stipulates that certain rights are inalienable or inviolable
variables.notes: Relevant to emergency_powers_constraints because inalienable rights cannot be suspended even during states of emergency

variables.description: Whether the constitution provides for an individual's right to self-determination or free development of personality
Whether there is a right to exemption from military service for conscientious objectors or other groups
Whether the constitution contains provisions for the protection of stateless individuals, refugees, or the right to asylum (includes prohibitions on extradition of political refugees)
Whether the constitution mentions the right to health care
Whether the constitution specifies that healthcare should be provided free of charge by government (asked only if HEALTHR=1)
Whether the constitution provides for matrimonial equality (equality within marriage; distinct from gender equality generally)
Whether the constitution provides the right for same-sex marriages
Whether the constitution guarantees the rights of children
